nodes.label: Vision: Every child achieving & thriving
nodes.text: Every child achieving and thriving — a country where the place a child grows up, from the North East to the coast, no longer sets a ceiling on how far they go.

nodes.label: Mission North East
nodes.text: Region-wide mission; phase 1 clusters in Sunderland and South Tyneside; phase 2 candidates Darlington and Middlesbrough.

nodes.label: Mission Coastal
nodes.text: Coastal mission; areas chosen by Ministers from a data-led shortlist: Scarborough (north) and Hastings (south).

nodes.label: Sunderland
nodes.text: Phase 1 Mission NE cluster. Sustained below-average KS4 attainment, among the highest disadvantaged underperformance in the region, elevated persistent absence, high deprivation (53rd most deprived nationally on IDACI), and no existing targeted DfE support (RISE) — genuine additionality.

nodes.label: South Tyneside
nodes.text: Phase 1 Mission NE cluster. A8 of 42.7 vs NE average 44.1 and national 46.0; disadvantaged A8 33.1, nearly two points below the national average; elevated persistent absence; 48th most deprived nationally on IDACI; not covered by RISE or other targeted programmes.

nodes.label: Scarborough
nodes.text: Mission Coastal (north). Chosen by Ministers from a data-led shortlist of coastal LADs. Disadvantaged A8 of 26.6 is among the weakest nationally. KS2 is notably weak: over half of primaries below national average, so the mission includes a strong primary attainment focus. Strong trusts (e.g. Delta) have recently taken on weak secondaries but pupil engagement remains the core challenge. Former Opportunity Area and Priority Education Investment Area.

nodes.label: Hastings
nodes.text: Mission Coastal (south). Disadvantaged A8 of 26.0 — the lowest of any coastal area in England for three years running. Defining challenge is churn: high pupil mobility driven by housing insecurity, plus a very large elective home education cohort (~10% of secondary-age pupils). Former Opportunity Area and PEIA.

nodes.label: Strand 1: Intensive School Improvement
nodes.text: Performance Support Teams of experienced leaders and specialists from high-performing trusts deployed into clusters of 6-8 secondary schools, working alongside local staff on attendance, behaviour, reading and KS3 catch-up. Practitioner-led, not consultancy.

nodes.label: Strand 2: Pupil Engagement & Enrichment
nodes.text: A structured enrichment and mentoring offer for every child in the mission areas, coordinated with the national Enrichment Framework and the DCMS £22.5m investment: sport, arts, careers, mentoring, work experience, delivered with anchor partners Right to Succeed, AllChild and Impetus.

nodes.label: Strand 3: Test, Learn & Grow
nodes.text: Place-based TLG projects, micro-RCTs and multi-agency pilots, scoped bottom-up with local partners and supported by a Cabinet Office-funded satellite team. The missions as the R&D arm of the English school system.

nodes.label: Strand 4: Leadership Capacity
nodes.text: Building the capacity and development of school leaders: place-led, focused on disadvantaged communities, with a long-term pipeline (Leadership Programmes North East plus philanthropic match).

nodes.label: The Efficacy Engine
nodes.text: Teacher × pupil × parental efficacy, connected by shared culture, strong relationships and accountability. In school, beyond the gate, centred on place.

nodes.label: White working-class attainment gap
nodes.text: Disadvantaged White British pupils average A8 30.9 vs 48.6 for non-disadvantaged peers: a 17.7-point gap within the same ethnic group. The generational injustice at the centre of the missions.

nodes.label: Performance Support Teams
nodes.text: ~25 specialists @ £800/day, ~120 days/yr, clusters of 6-8 secondaries. £2.4m/yr: the largest investment.

nodes.label: Enrichment Framework + DCMS £22.5m
nodes.text: National Enrichment Framework rollout in mission areas, complemented by DCMS enrichment funding.

nodes.label: Real-time data dashboard
nodes.text: £150k/yr for dashboard, impact evaluation and research partnership.

nodes.label: Right to Succeed
nodes.text: Anchor / funding partner.

nodes.label: AllChild
nodes.text: Anchor / funding partner.

nodes.label: Impetus
nodes.text: Anchor / funding partner.

nodes.label: Mission 44
nodes.text: Anchor / funding partner.

nodes.label: DCMS
nodes.text: Anchor / funding partner.

nodes.label: SHINE
nodes.text: Anchor / funding partner.

nodes.label: WhatWorked Education
nodes.text: Anchor / funding partner.

nodes.label: NECA
nodes.text: Anchor / funding partner.

nodes.label: Cabinet Office TLG team
nodes.text: Anchor / funding partner.

nodes.label: National Rapid Evidence Network
nodes.text: [cross-cutting] Micro-trial infrastructure in mission schools growing into an opt-in rapid evidence network any school in England can join.

nodes.label: Child Engagement Early Warning System
nodes.text: [cross-cutting] Join DfE attendance with NHS, social care and youth service signals into a single safeguarding-grade view per child, with predictive flags tested at the Y6-7 transition and a tested human response paired to every flag.

nodes.label: Family Engagement Guarantee
nodes.text: [cross-cutting] Trials of structured parental engagement: text-nudges, community parent mentors, family learning, reverse parents’ evenings — targeting white working-class families reporting the lowest trust in schools.

nodes.label: Enrichment Passport & outcome-linked commissioning
nodes.text: [cross-cutting] A digital enrichment passport for every mission pupil; providers paid partly on verified participation of disadvantaged pupils.

nodes.label: The Year 7 Bridge
nodes.text: [cross-cutting] The Y6-7 transition as a designed experience: summer bridge on secondary sites, one childhood one record, KS3 engagement curricula, transition mentors from Y9-10.

nodes.label: Inclusion Labs
nodes.text: [cross-cutting] Volunteer mission schools test inclusive mainstream models the SEND reform programme needs evidence for.

nodes.label: KS3 Micro-Trial Partnership
nodes.text: [Sunderland & South Tyneside] Deepen the WhatWorked Education partnership into the country’s first secondary classroom-practice trial unit embedded in PST cluster schools; every PST intervention becomes a trial arm.

nodes.label: “Made in the North East” industry pathways
nodes.text: [North East] Employer-embedded enrichment at scale using advanced manufacturing, electrification, offshore energy and Crown Works Studios: workplace encounters from Y7, employer-sponsored KS3 projects, technical tasters.

nodes.label: “Every Mover Known”
nodes.text: [Hastings] Rapid-integration protocol for mid-year movers (48-hour records transfer, automatic enrichment and mentoring enrolment, named integration lead) plus a coastal workforce premium trial.

nodes.label: “The Year-Round Coast”
nodes.text: [Scarborough] Year-round opportunity model for a seasonal economy: holiday enrichment, paid summer roles with tourism/offshore wind/marine sectors, a coastal curriculum, school-year scheduling flexibilities.

nodes.label: Attendance & persistent absence
nodes.text: Secondary PA: South Tyneside 28.2%, Sunderland 28.4%, Hastings 31.9% vs England 23.4%. Daily monitoring, family liaison and PA protocols are core tactics.

nodes.label: NEET & destinations
nodes.text: 16-17 NEET-or-not-known: Sunderland 6.7%, South Tyneside 5.6% vs England 5.6%. Fewer NEETs and stronger post-16 progression is a mission objective.

nodes.label: Primary attainment (KS2)
nodes.text: Scarborough RWM 57% vs national ~61-62%; over half of MCS primaries below national average. Maths is the binding constraint; the girls’ advantage has vanished.

nodes.label: Pupil mobility & churn
nodes.text: Hastings: ~10% of secondary-age pupils in elective home education, 928 (14.7%) out of or at risk of being out of provision. Housing insecurity drives mid-year moves.

nodes.label: Seasonal coastal economy
nodes.text: Scarborough: disadvantage concentrates in winter, disengagement in summer. Biggest employer Anglo American; rest mostly tourism and hospitality.

nodes.label: Parental engagement
nodes.text: White working-class families report the lowest trust in schools: the single biggest evidence gap named in the missions’ TLG remit.

nodes.label: Y6-7 transition
nodes.text: Risk concentrates at the Year 6 to 7 transition where data fractures across systems.

nodes.label: Governance: Partnership Board + Operational Boards
nodes.text: Governance board chaired by the Minister; local operational boards for each mission underneath. Youth Advisory Board for Mission NE.

nodes.label: £4.2m annual DfE envelope
nodes.text: Covers both missions; philanthropic and partner contributions handled in a parallel workstream.
